creator: destination_wellknown
caption:
Copenhageners are invading this Danish town 👇  I’m starting a new travel series where I’ll be exploring the best places in Denmark outside of Copenhagen (don’t worry, I’ll still make plenty of Copenhagen content) 🇩🇰

So why are people so crazy about Svendborg? 

Well, it’s a very pretty little town and the nature surrounding it is absolutely gorgeous. But the thing that strikes me the most is how culturally rich Svendborg is 🙌

Cities in Denmark outside of Copenhagen (and perhaps Aarhus, the 2nd city) can be quite dull where not much is happening. That’s definitely not the case in Svendborg; there are loads of concerts, summer festivals and a yearly film festival ⚡️ 

A few of my favourite places & things to do in Svendborg include: 

🎸 Spillestedet Harders (cool concert venue run by volunteers)
🍺 Hansted (best bar in town) 👨🏻‍🍳 105 bar & køkken (trendy restaurant/bar) 🍺 Kammerateriet (outdoor hangout spot by the harbour) 🥖 Hello Banh Mi (great little Vietnamese restaurant) 👨🏻‍🍳 Restaurant Resumé (extreme value for money fine dining) ⛵️ Helge (100-year-old sailboat that does daily tours) ☕️/🍷 Café Rouge (french-style café/bar)  What do you think about the look of Svendborg? And do you have anywhere in Denmark you’d like me to visit? 😎  #svendborg #visitdenmark #denmarktravel #traveltips #travelguide

transcription.text: exploring the best places in Denmark outside of Copenhagen. Welcome to Svendborg. This charming Danish town is almost 800 years old and used to be the Danish town with the most pups per citizen. It's located in the southern part of the island of Funen where the biggest city is Odense but Odense is one of the most boring places in Denmark so avoid that and get the train down here. Svendborg surely is one of Denmark's best smaller towns with just shy of 30 000 inhabitants and it's the gateway to a lot of stunning islands so come here for a night or two and make sure you go on a boat tour around the islands.
